ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE

ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT HER HEALTH IS SUCH THAT PREFERENCE IS NECESSARY TO PREVENT PREJUDICING HER INTEREST IN THE LITIGATION, AS REQUIRED BY C.C.P. 36(A). PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF HER MOTION CONTRADICTS HER FEBRUARY 10, 2017 DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. (D'AMICO V. BOARD OF MEDICAL EXAMINERS (1974) 11 CAL.3D 1.) HER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS GIVEN MORE WEIGHT THAN HER DECLARATION AND IT DEMONSTRATES THAT PREFERENCE IS NOT NECESSARY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, REPORTER: SANDY LEE OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 	971 (503)

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Emma Ward, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of William Ward, Deceased.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial and extending discovery cutoff is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate that her health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing her interest in the litigation, as required by C.C.P. 36(a). Plaintiff's declaration submitted in support of her motion contradicts her February 10, 2017 deposition testimony. (D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1.) Her deposition testimony is given more weight than her declaration and it demonstrates that preference is not necessary. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
